title: Adoption risks + next product-quality improvement for pilot expansion
summary: Onboarding completion improved after the docs refresh, but adoption is still constrained by setup/credential failure states and unclear recovery. Pilot expansion is explicitly gated on making failed-install recovery self-serve and on improving trust signals in generated recommendations via traceable citations and labeled evidence sources.

claims.text: Quickstart completion increased from 44% to 58% after the docs copy refresh, and exits decreased at the first API-call step, but the largest remaining drop occurs during local setup and credential configuration due to environment-specific errors.

Completion gains were strongest for users who already had a valid token; new users without a configured environment still hit the same setup wall, and the team cautioned against treating the metric as end-to-end onboarding success until credential setup has a recovery path.

Support tickets from new users most often involve environment drift, stale CLI versions, missing environment variables, and credentials copied from the wrong workspace; users frequently lack clear recovery steps after failed installs.
An internal support checklist resolves most setup cases, but key steps are not in public docs, and tickets sometimes reopen when users restart the quickstart and reproduce the same broken state.

Pilot interviewees trust recommendations only when each claim links back to inspectable source snippets; they prefer evidence separated by origin (analytics vs support vs interviews) over blended rationales.

openQuestions: Which specific setup error clusters (by OS/shell/CI vs local) account for the highest bounce during credential configuration, and can we prioritize the top 2–3 with targeted recovery flows? (S1)
What is the minimal public-facing recovery checklist that matches the internal support checklist, and which steps are currently “support-only” knowledge? (S2)
What should the product’s observable “citation checks” and evidence-origin labeling look like in the lab to meet pilot prerequisites? (S4,S6)
recommendation: Prioritize a self-serve setup recovery path as the next product-quality improvement before expanding the pilot: publish and integrate a public recovery checklist covering stale CLI version detection/upgrade, environment variable verification, workspace credential mismatch, and safe reset/retry from a partial install (including explicit ‘what state am I in’ diagnostics). This directly targets the biggest onboarding drop (local setup/credentials) and the top support drivers, and it is the explicit gate for scheduling additional pilot teams without relying on support pasting an internal checklist. In parallel (but second), implement trace-backed recommendations that display source citations and label whether evidence came from analytics/support/interviews to satisfy stated trust and readiness gates. (S1,S2,S4,S5,S6,S3)
